algorithm.title: Algorithm Overview: Weng-Lin (OpenSkill)
algorithm.intro: Weng-Lin is a Bayesian online ranking algorithm published by Ruby C. Weng and Chih-Jen Lin in 2011. It is functionally equivalent to TrueSkill but uses logistic distribution instead of Gaussian, computes updates via closed-form equations, is 2.5-6.5x faster, and is not patented.
algorithm.params_sigma: Standard deviation of the skill estimate
key_properties: Expected outcome → Ω ≈ 0 → ratings barely change
σ always decreases (system becomes more confident)
Higher σ → larger updates (more to learn)
algorithm.beta_desc: Our rating scale is [0, ~3000] instead of the default [0, 50]. The ratio β/σ₀ remains similar: 200/333 ≈ 0.6 vs 4.17/8.33 ≈ 0.5. The inflated scale provides leaderboard-friendly integer values.
algorithm.winning_team_label: For each player i on the winning team:
algorithm.losing_team_label: For each player j on the losing team:

architecture.title: Processing Pipeline
architecture.desc: The wrapper sits on top of weng_lin_two_teams() and adds AG-specific logic. The crate handles the Bayesian math; the wrapper handles game-specific adjustments.
architecture.step1_desc: Asserts that teams are not empty to prevent NaN propagation from division by zero.
architecture.step2_desc: Transforms PlayerMatchData (rating, uncertainty, frags, deaths) into WengLinRating { rating: μ, uncertainty: σ }.
architecture.step3_desc: Calls weng_lin_two_teams(blue, red, outcome, config) which computes μ_new and σ_new per player using the closed-form Weng-Lin equations.
architecture.step4_desc: See The AG Wrapper Logic section below for the detailed breakdown of each adjustment applied by the wrapper.
architecture.step4a_hint: The raw difference between the Weng-Lin result and the player’s previous rating.
architecture.step4b_hint: Calculates individual performance bonus/penalty using the efficiency metric, comparing the player to their team average.
architecture.step4c_hint: Combines the Bayesian base delta with the performance bonus for the final unclamped adjustment.
architecture.step4d_hint: For winners: if final_delta is too small, it is raised to a minimum floor. engagement_scale = clamp((frags + deaths) / 20, 0.1, 1.0)
architecture.step4e_hint: The final rating is clamped to ensure it never drops below the configured rating_floor.
architecture.step4f_hint: A dynamics variance (tau) is added to prevent sigma from collapsing to zero over many matches.

post_bayesian.title: The AG Wrapper Logic
post_bayesian.desc: The raw Weng-Lin output is just the beginning. Our wrapper applies 6 sequential adjustments to account for individual performance and AG-specific gameplay:

v1.title: v1 — Direct Weng-Lin (initial)
v1.desc: Initial implementation using weng_lin_two_teams() directly from the skillratings crate without any wrapper. No AG-specific adjustments: no performance modifier, no minimum delta, no rating floor.

v2.title: v2 — Multiplicative Wrapper
v2.desc: Added the AG Rating Wrapper with a multiplicative performance modifier. final_delta = base_delta × modifier for winners, final_delta = base_delta / modifier for losers. Performance measured as raw K/D: frags - deaths × weight.

v3.title: v3 — Additive Wrapper (current)
v3.desc: Replaced multiplicative with additive: final_delta = base_delta + perf_bonus. Performance metric changed to efficiency: (frags - deaths) / max(frags + deaths, 1). Added sigma scaling for faster convergence of new players. Winner engagement floor scaled by participation.

banner.dev_warning: This system is in heavy development. Ratings and data may change at any time.
